        "Identify decimal expansions as terminating, recurring, or non-recurring.",
        "Represent square roots geometrically on the number line.",
        "Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers in practical contexts."

      ],

      "questions": [
        {
          "id": "q1",
          "questionNumber": 1,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "Which of the following is a rational number?",
          "options": [
            "A. √2",
            "B. π",
            "C. 0.75",
            "D. 0.1010010001..."
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Define Real Numbers as the union of Rational and Irrational numbers."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Rational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Rational vs Irrational"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "understanding",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Rational numbers are fractions or decimals that end or repeat."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q2",
          "questionNumber": 2,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "The decimal expansion of the number √3 is:",
          "options": [
            "A. Terminating",
            "B. Non-terminating recurring",
            "C. Non-terminating non-recurring",
            "D. Finite"
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Identify decimal expansions as terminating, recurring, or non-recurring."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Irrational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Decimal Expansions"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "understanding",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Irrational numbers have non-terminating, non-recurring decimal expansions."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q3",
          "questionNumber": 3,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "mcq",
          "subtype": "mcq",
          "prompt": "Which of the following is an irrational number?",
          "options": [
            "A. 22/7",
            "B. 0.5",
            "C. √9",
            "D. π"
          ],
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Define Real Numbers as the union of Rational and Irrational numbers."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Irrational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Rational vs Irrational"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "recall",
          "competencyTag": "Conceptual clarity",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: π is an example of an irrational number."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q4",
          "questionNumber": 4,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "veryShortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Is the number 0.123456789... (where the pattern of digits increases) rational or irrational?",
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "One word or short phrase",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Identify decimal expansions as terminating, recurring, or non-recurring."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Irrational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Decimal Expansions"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "understanding",
          "competencyTag": "Analysis",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Non-terminating non-recurring decimals are irrational."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q5",
          "questionNumber": 5,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "veryShortAnswer",
          "subtype": "veryShortAnswer",
          "prompt": "What is the value of √9?",
          "marks": 1,
          "expectedLength": "One word or short phrase",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers in practical contexts."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Rational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Square Roots"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "recall",
          "competencyTag": "Application",
          "difficulty": "easy",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: √9 = 3, which is a rational number."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q6",
          "questionNumber": 6,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Explain why the sum of a rational number and an irrational number is always irrational.",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "2-3 sentences",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Define Real Numbers as the union of Rational and Irrational numbers."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Real Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Properties of Real Numbers"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "analysis",
          "competencyTag": "Reasoning",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: The sum of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q7",
          "questionNumber": 7,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Describe the steps to locate √2 on the number line using geometric construction.",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "Step-by-step description",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Represent square roots geometrically on the number line."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Geometric Representation"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Square Roots"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Application",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Draw a right-angled triangle with base 1 and height 1 to find hypotenuse √2."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q8",
          "questionNumber": 8,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "A square has a side length of 1 meter. What is the length of its diagonal? Is this number rational or irrational?",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "Value and classification",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Represent square roots geometrically on the number line.",
            "Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers in practical contexts."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Geometric Representation",
            "Irrational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Square Roots"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "application",
          "competencyTag": "Application",
          "difficulty": "medium",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: The diagonal of a square with side 1 is √2. It cannot be written as p/q."
          ]
        },
        {
          "id": "q9",
          "questionNumber": 9,
          "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
          "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
          "type": "shortAnswer",
          "subtype": "shortAnswer",
          "prompt": "Identify the type of decimal expansion for the number 0.1010010001... and justify your answer.",
          "marks": 2,
          "expectedLength": "Classification and justification",
          "learningOutcomeRefs": [
            "Identify decimal expansions as terminating, recurring, or non-recurring."
          ],
          "conceptRefs": [
            "Irrational Numbers"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "subtopicCoverage": [
            "Decimal Expansions"
          ],
          "bloomsLevel": "analysis",
          "competencyTag": "Analysis",
          "difficulty": "hard",
          "sourceEvidence": [
            "Teacher notes: Non-terminating non-recurring decimals are irrational."
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "evaluation": {
      "answerKey": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q1",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "C. 0.75",
            "explanation": "0.75 is a terminating decimal, which can be written as a fraction (3/4).",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q2",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "C. Non-terminating non-recurring",
            "explanation": "√3 is an irrational number, so its decimal expansion goes on forever without repeating.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q3",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "D. π",
            "explanation": "π is a classic example of an irrational number.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "mcq"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q4",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "Irrational",
            "explanation": "The pattern increases and does not repeat, making it non-terminating and non-recurring.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "veryShortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q5",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "3",
            "explanation": "√9 = 3, which is a rational number.",
            "marks": 1,
            "subtype": "veryShortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q6",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "If the sum were rational, subtracting the known rational number would result in a rational irrational number, which is a contradiction.",
            "explanation": "The sum of a rational and an irrational number cannot be rational.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q7",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "1. Draw a horizontal line and mark 0 and 1. 2. Construct a perpendicular at 1 of length 1. 3. Join the origin to the top of the perpendicular (hypotenuse = √2). 4. Use a compass to mark this length on the number line.",
            "explanation": "Geometric construction uses the Pythagorean theorem where base=1, height=1, hypotenuse=√2.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q8",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "Length is √2 meters. It is irrational.",
            "explanation": "By Pythagoras theorem, diagonal = √(1² + 1²) = √2. √2 cannot be written as p/q.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "sectionId": "section-unit-i-number-system",
            "sectionTitle": "Unit I: Number System",
            "answer": "Non-terminating non-recurring (Irrational). The pattern of zeros increases, so it never repeats.",
            "explanation": "Since the decimal does not terminate and does not repeat a fixed pattern, it is irrational.",
            "marks": 2,
            "subtype": "shortAnswer"
          }
        ]
      },

              {

      "rubrics": {
        "items": [
          {
            "questionId": "q6",
            "totalMarks": 2,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "First valid point (Statement of property)",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Second valid point (Logical reasoning)",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "The sum is irrational because if it were rational, subtracting the rational part would make the irrational number rational, which is impossible."
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q7",
            "totalMarks": 2,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "First valid point (Triangle construction)",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Second valid point (Compass transfer)",
                "marks": 1

            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "Draw a right triangle with base 1 and height 1. The hypotenuse is √2. Use a compass to mark this length on the number line."
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q8",
            "totalMarks": 2,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "First valid point (Calculation of diagonal)",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Second valid point (Classification as irrational)",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "Diagonal = √(1²+1²) = √2. Since √2 cannot be written as p/q, it is irrational."
          },
          {
            "questionId": "q9",
            "totalMarks": 2,
            "criteria": [
              {
                "criterion": "First valid point (Correct identification)",
                "marks": 1
              },
              {
                "criterion": "Second valid point (Justification of non-repeating pattern)",
                "marks": 1
              }
            ],
            "sampleAnswer": "It is non-terminating non-recurring because the number of zeros between ones keeps increasing, so no pattern repeats."
          }
        ]
      },
